_source: PMBOK Guide 7th Edition, Scrum Guide, SAFe framework, PMI-ACP materials
_note: Long-tail project management. Agile ceremonies, risk management, PMP processes, stakeholder management.

roles.product_owner: Owns product vision and backlog; prioritizes features; accepts completed work; voice of customer
roles.scrum_master: Facilitates process; removes blockers; coaches team; protects from interruptions; not a manager
roles.development_team: Cross-functional; self-organizing; 3-9 people; delivers working increments

sprint_planning.when: Start of sprint; 2-4 hours for 2-week sprint
sprint_planning.outputs: Sprint backlog; commitment to sprint goal
sprint_planning.questions: What can we deliver? How will we do it?

daily_standup.when: Daily; same time/place; 15 minutes max; stand to keep it short
daily_standup.format: Each person: yesterday's work, today's plan, blockers
daily_standup.not: Status report to manager; problem-solving session (take offline)

sprint_review.purpose: Demo working software to stakeholders; gather feedback
sprint_review.outcome: Product backlog updates based on feedback

sprint_retrospective.when: After review; end of sprint; 1-1.5 hours
sprint_retrospective.purpose: Team reflects on process; what went well, what to improve, action items

backlog_refinement.purpose: Break down user stories; estimate; clarify acceptance criteria; 2-3 sprints ahead
backlog_refinement.goal: 10-15% of team's time; stories 'ready' for sprint planning

artifacts.product_backlog: Prioritized list of features; user stories; epics; owned by PO
artifacts.sprint_backlog: User stories committed for current sprint + tasks
artifacts.increment: Sum of completed backlog items; potentially shippable; meets DoD
artifacts.burndown_chart: Work remaining vs time; ideal trend line vs actual; shows progress

definition_of_done.purpose: Shared understanding of 'complete'; quality gate; prevents undone work

kanban.principles: Visualize work; limit WIP; manage flow; make policies explicit; feedback loops; improve collaboratively
kanban.wip_limits: Maximum items in each column; prevents overload; forces finishing before starting; typical 1-2× team size

flow_metrics.cycle_time: Time from start to done for single item; lower is better
flow_metrics.throughput: Items completed per time period; higher is better
flow_metrics.wip: Work in progress; lower WIP usually improves cycle time (Little's Law)

kanban.board_structure: To Do | In Progress (WIP limit 3) | Code Review (WIP limit 2) | Done

safe_scaled_agile.art: Agile Release Train; 5-12 teams; aligned to common cadence and vision; 8-12 week PI
safe_scaled_agile.pi_planning: Program Increment planning; all teams; 2 days; plan 8-12 weeks; dependencies identified; team commitments
safe_scaled_agile.inspect_and_adapt: End of PI; demo, quantitative metrics review, retrospective, improvement backlog

user_story_writing.format: As a [role], I want [feature], so that [benefit]

invest_criteria.independent: Minimal dependencies on other stories; can be developed in any order
invest_criteria.negotiable: Details negotiable; not a contract; conversation over documentation
invest_criteria.valuable: Delivers value to user/business; not technical tasks
invest_criteria.estimable: Team can estimate size; if not, needs refinement or spike
invest_criteria.small: Completable in one sprint; split if too large; 1-5 days ideal
invest_criteria.testable: Clear acceptance criteria; can verify done

acceptance_criteria.example: Given user logged in, when click 'Export', then CSV downloads with all visible data
acceptance_criteria.purpose: Defines done; enables testing; prevents misunderstanding

story_splitting.avoid: Splitting by layer (frontend/backend); want vertical slices delivering value

story_points.definition: Relative size; complexity + effort + uncertainty; unitless; not hours
story_points.fibonacci_scale: 1, 2, 3, 5, 8, 13, 21; forces coarse estimates; diminishing precision at larger sizes
story_points.planning_poker: Team estimates simultaneously; discuss outliers; converge on estimate; prevents anchoring

velocity.calculation: Average story points completed per sprint over last 3-5 sprints
velocity.use: Forecast completion; 'we do 30 points/sprint, 120 points remaining = 4 sprints'
velocity.not: Comparison between teams; performance metric for individuals; changes over time with team changes

t_shirt_sizing.scale: XS, S, M, L, XL; coarse estimate for early planning
t_shirt_sizing.conversion: XS=1, S=2, M=5, L=8, XL=13 story points approximately
t_shirt_sizing.use: Epics and features; refine to story points closer to sprint

identify_risks.sources: Brainstorming; lessons learned; stakeholder interviews; assumptions; dependencies
identify_risks.categories: Technical; schedule; cost; resource; external; regulatory; market

qualitative_analysis.risk_matrix: High prob + high impact = critical; low prob + low impact = accept

quantitative_analysis.monte_carlo: Simulate 1000s iterations; probability distribution of outcomes; requires software
quantitative_analysis.decision_tree: Expected Monetary Value = Probability × Impact for each branch; choose highest EMV

response_strategies.avoid: Eliminate threat; change plan; prove technology with POC before committing
response_strategies.mitigate: Reduce probability or impact; add buffer; extra testing; redundant suppliers
response_strategies.transfer: Shift to third party; insurance; fixed-price contract; warranty
response_strategies.accept: Acknowledge but don't act; contingency reserve for if it occurs
response_strategies.escalate: Outside PM authority; escalate to sponsor; regulatory risks
response_strategies.opportunities: Exploit (ensure it happens); Enhance (increase probability); Share (with partner); Accept

contingency_reserve.purpose: Budget/schedule buffer for known unknowns (identified risks)
contingency_reserve.sizing: 10-20% of budget typical; risk-adjusted based on analysis
contingency_reserve.management_reserve: Separate from contingency; for unknown unknowns; requires sponsor approval to use

power_interest_grid.high_power_high_interest: Manage closely; frequent updates; key decision makers
power_interest_grid.high_power_low_interest: Keep satisfied; don't overload with detail; executives
power_interest_grid.low_power_high_interest: Keep informed; willing supporters; team members, users

identification.salience_model: Power, Legitimacy, Urgency; stakeholders with all three are 'definitive' (highest priority)

engagement_strategies.resistant: Aware but opposed; understand objections; address concerns; involve in decisions; WIIFM (what's in it for me)
engagement_strategies.neutral: Aware but uncommitted; educate on benefits; demonstrate quick wins
engagement_strategies.supportive: Aware and supportive; leverage as champions; amplify their voice
engagement_strategies.leading: Actively engaged and promoting; empower; give visibility

communication_plan.elements: Who (stakeholder); What (info); When (frequency); How (medium); Who sends
communication_plan.example: Steering committee; project status; monthly; presentation; PM sends
communication_plan.tailor: Executives want summary; team wants detail; customers want benefits; adjust message and medium

initiating.develop_project_charter: Formal authorization; high-level scope, milestones, budget; sponsor approval
initiating.identify_stakeholders: Who's affected or can influence; power/interest grid; engagement strategy

planning.scope_management: Collect requirements; define scope; create WBS; scope baseline
planning.schedule_management: Define activities; sequence; estimate durations; develop schedule; critical path
planning.cost_management: Estimate costs; determine budget; cost baseline
planning.quality_management: Plan quality; define metrics (defect rate <2%); quality assurance and control
planning.resource_management: Estimate resources; acquire team; team charter; RACI matrix
planning.communications_management: Who needs what info when; distribution list; cadence
planning.risk_management: Identify, analyze, plan responses; risk register
planning.procurement_management: Make or buy; RFP; vendor selection criteria

executing.direct_and_manage: Lead team; implement plan; manage changes
executing.manage_quality: Audits; process improvements; follow quality plan
executing.acquire_resources: Hire or assign team; procure materials
executing.conduct_procurements: Solicit bids; select vendors; negotiate contracts

monitoring_and_controlling.monitor_and_control: Track performance; compare to baseline; variance analysis
monitoring_and_controlling.perform_integrated_change_control: Change requests; CCB approval; update baselines
monitoring_and_controlling.control_schedule: Analyze variance; forecast completion; take corrective action
monitoring_and_controlling.control_costs: EV analysis; CPI, SPI; forecast EAC
monitoring_and_controlling.control_quality: Inspect deliverables; root cause analysis; defect fixes

closing.close_project: Final deliverable acceptance; release resources; lessons learned; celebrate; archive

key_metrics.pv: Planned Value; budgeted cost of scheduled work; baseline
key_metrics.ev: Earned Value; budgeted cost of completed work; what you got
key_metrics.ac: Actual Cost; actual cost of completed work; what you paid
key_metrics.bac: Budget at Completion; total planned budget

variance.cv: Cost Variance = EV - AC; positive is under budget; negative is over budget
variance.sv: Schedule Variance = EV - PV; positive is ahead; negative is behind

performance_indices.cpi: Cost Performance Index = EV / AC; >1.0 is under budget; <1.0 is over budget; 1.0 is on budget
performance_indices.spi: Schedule Performance Index = EV / PV; >1.0 is ahead; <1.0 is behind

forecasting.eac: Estimate at Completion = BAC / CPI (if current trends continue); or AC + (BAC - EV) if atypical variance
forecasting.etc: Estimate to Complete = EAC - AC; how much more we'll spend
forecasting.vac: Variance at Completion = BAC - EAC; final expected variance

example.scenario: Project 50% complete; PV=$100k, EV=$80k, AC=$90k, BAC=$200k
example.analysis: CV = 80-90 = -$10k (over budget); SV = 80-100 = -$20k (behind schedule); CPI = 80/90 = 0.89; SPI = 80/100 = 0.8
example.forecast: EAC = 200/0.89 = $225k; project will finish $25k over budget if trends continue

critical_path_method.process: Sequence activities; estimate durations; draw network diagram; calculate early/late dates; identify critical path
critical_path_method.critical_path: Longest path through network; zero float; determines minimum project duration; delay on CP delays project
critical_path_method.float_slack: Total Float = LS - ES (or LF - EF); amount activity can be delayed without delaying project
critical_path_method.fast_tracking: Perform activities in parallel normally done in sequence; increases risk
critical_path_method.crashing: Add resources to shorten duration; increases cost; crash activities on critical path only
